cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The problem gives me a table of penguins with their name, age, height, and weight. I need to figure out how many penguins are less than 8 years old and weigh less than 12 kg. The options are from A to E, so I need to check each penguin against those two conditions. First, let me list out all the penguins and their details again to make sure I have them right: 1. Louis: age 7, height 50 cm, weight 11 kg 2. Bernard: age 5, height 80 cm, weight 13 kg 3. Vincent: age 9, height 60 cm, weight 11 kg 4. Gwen: age 8, height 70 cm, weight 15 kg Now the conditions: age < 8 and weight < 12 kg. Both conditions need to be true for a penguin to count. Let me check each one: - Louis: age 7 (which is less than 8), weight 11 kg (less than 12). So both conditions met. Count 1. - Bernard: age 5 (less than 8), weight 13 kg (which is more than 12). So weight doesn't meet. Doesn't count. - Vincent: age 9 (which is more than 8, so condition fails). Even though weight is 11 kg, age is over 8. Doesn't count. - Gwen: age 8 (not less than 8, so condition fails). Weight is 15 kg. Doesn't count. So only Louis meets both conditions. Wait, but let me double-check. Louis is 7 and 11 kg. Yes. Bernard is 5 but 13 kg. So only one penguin. So the answer is A) 1. Wait, but let me check again. The age has to be less than 8, so under 8. So 7 and 5 are okay. But for weight less than 12.
